rows.requirement: Compiler code can propagate recoverable Option and Result failures without backend-specific exceptions.
rows.validatingCommand: make stage1-conformance && cargo test --manifest-path stage1/Cargo.toml -p axiomc --test cranelift_backend cranelift_backend_lowers_nested_result_match_to_runtime_exit_code -- --nocapture
rows.notes: Postfix ? and Option/Result payload behavior are documented and covered by conformance plus direct-native backend tests.

rows.requirement: Compiler code can model scalar values, tuples, arrays, maps, structs, enums, Option and Result payloads, and ownership-sensitive aggregate movement.
rows.validatingCommand: make stage1-conformance && cargo test --manifest-path stage1/Cargo.toml -p axiomc --test cranelift_backend cranelift_backend_lowers_result_struct_payload_match_to_runtime_exit_code -- --nocapture
rows.notes: This is sufficient for the current checklist row; larger compiler-scale data structure use is tracked separately by the compiler rewrite row.

rows.requirement: Compiler code can allocate, grow, index, mutate, and iterate runtime-sized collections with explicit ownership and failure behavior.
rows.notes: Fixed literal arrays and scalar index mutation are implemented, but compiler-scale workloads still lack runtime-sized allocation and growth.

rows.requirement: Compiler packages can pass runtime strings, immutable slices, and mutable slices across direct-native function boundaries with defined ownership, length, aliasing, and write-through behavior.
rows.validatingCommand: make stage1-direct-native-runtime-abi && make stage1-conformance && make self-hosting-spike-parity
rows.notes: Local loop and mutable-array behavior is implemented; cross-function runtime string and slice ABI behavior remains incomplete.

rows.requirement: Compiler support libraries can use explicit generics and static trait-bounded dispatch where a compiler package needs reusable typed helpers.
rows.notes: Static bounded generics are in scope; dynamic dispatch remains intentionally outside this readiness row.

rows.requirement: Compiler code can build strings, inspect textual input, format diagnostics, and use deterministic JSON and regex helpers without host-language escapes.
rows.notes: This row covers the text tooling needed for lexer, parser, diagnostics, and machine-readable reports; the diagnostics spike now uses direct string_contains instead of a regex workaround.

rows.requirement: Compiler command code can access scoped files, environment, clocks, process status, loopback networking, crypto, JSON, and regex through capability-gated AxiOM stdlib surfaces.
rows.notes: Rows stay capability-scoped; they do not grant unrestricted host access.

rows.requirement: Compiler packages can be split across modules, local dependencies, and workspace members with deterministic lockfile validation.
rows.validatingCommand: make stage1-conformance && cargo test --manifest-path stage1/Cargo.toml -p axiomc --lib package_graph -- --nocapture
rows.notes: This proves the language/package surface, not that the Rust compiler source has already been ported.

rows.requirement: A self-hosted compiler can expose check, build, run, test, doc, and LSP-facing workflows through AxiOM-owned command packages.
rows.notes: Language helpers exist, but the final bootstrap track still needs AxiOM-owned command-package proof before the compiler rewrite can start.

rows.requirement: At least one compiler-scale AxiOM package exercises parser, checker, diagnostics, package graph, and codegen-style data flow before the rewrite starts.
rows.notes: This row prevents language-readiness from being inferred from small examples alone.
